title: Heatwave
body: Extreme heat is a universal health risk. Use these accessible, low-cost strategies to regulate your body temperature and stay safe.
tips: Drink tap water regularly; do not wait for thirst.
Seek shade or public cooling spaces like libraries or mosques.
Use wet cloths on wrists, neck, and armpits to cool down quickly.
Wear loose, light-colored clothing to reduce heat absorption.
Watch for symptoms like confusion or dizziness (medical emergency).

title: Hot Weather
body: High temperatures detected. Risk of heat stress during outdoor exposure.
tips: Use fans or cooling areas when possible.
Take frequent breaks if working outside.

title: Storm
body: Strong winds detected. Outdoor objects may become dangerous.
tips: Stay indoors and away from windows.

title: Extreme Storm
body: Dangerous wind conditions detected. Risk of structural damage and flying debris.
tips: Move away from glass and windows.

title: Thunderstorm
body: Lightning activity detected. High risk of electric discharge.
tips: Stay away from water and plumbing.
Wait 30 minutes after last thunder before going out.

title: Flood Risk
body: Heavy rainfall may lead to flash flooding. Water levels can rise quickly.
tips: Never walk or drive through floodwater.
Disconnect electrical devices if water enters home.

title: Heavy Rain
body: Strong rainfall detected. Possible water accumulation and reduced visibility.

title: Fog Alert
tips: Pull over if visibility becomes too low.

title: Cold Wave
body: Low temperature conditions detected. Risk of hypothermia.
tips: Eat warm meals and drink hot fluids.

title: Freezing Cold
body: Extreme cold conditions detected. High risk of frostbite and hypothermia.
tips: Seek shelter immediately if symptoms appear.

title: Snow
body: Snowfall detected. Travel and movement may be difficult.
tips: Shovel snow safely in small amounts.

title: Earthquake
body: Earthquake detected. Immediate protective action required.
tips: Stay away from windows and heavy objects.
If outdoors, move to open area.
If in car, stop safely and stay inside.
